text:
Once upon a time in the quaint town of Willow Creek stood an ancient cherry blossom tree, renowned across the land for its vibrant bloom. Every spring, it'd paint the town square in a cascade of pink. Little Sara, who loved nothing more than spending hours beneath its shade, noticed one springday that the tree seemed unusually frail.

She would often ponder why it hadn't bloomed that year. One chilly morning, while admiring its silent beauty, she spotted a soft, furry creature perched on a low branch. It appeared to be a fox, but something about its odd coat and glimmering eyes didn't seem quite right.

Curiosity piqued, Sara drew nearer, stepping gingerly through the dewy grass. The fox let out a low whistle, attracting a gang of barn owls that flew down in a circle around them. Sensing danger—a bunny alone should not incite a nocturnal ambush—they retreated quickly, leaving the fox wandering.

Sara could hear it
